user_request:
I want to create a table tracking AI companies that are actively hiring.

Columns needed:
- Company Name
- Website
- Is hiring for AI roles? (yes/no)
- Team size (approximate)
- Recent funding (last round)

Find about 10 companies across different AI sectors like research, healthcare, and enterprise.
Make sure to include both established companies and startups.

columns.name: Company Name
columns.description: The official name of the AI company as registered or publicly recognized. Used to uniquely identify the organization.

columns.name: Website
columns.description: The primary website or careers page of the company, providing direct access to information about the organization and its job openings.
columns.validation_strategy: Verify that the URL is active and leads to the official company homepage or dedicated careers page. Confirm that the site is legitimate and not a third-party aggregator.

columns.name: Is hiring for AI roles?
columns.description: Indicates whether the company is actively hiring for AI-related positions as of Q4 2024–Q1 2025. Includes both technical (e.g., ML engineer, data scientist) and non-technical (e.g., AI product manager, AI strategist) roles.
columns.validation_strategy: Check the company's official careers page, recent job postings (within the last 3 months), and reputable job boards for open AI-related positions. Confirm that at least one AI-specific role is listed and open for applications. Reference recent hiring trend reports for additional confirmation if needed.[1][3][7][11]

columns.name: Team size (approximate)
columns.description: Estimated total number of employees at the company, reflecting its organizational scale. For startups, use the most recent headcount disclosed in press releases, company profiles, or funding announcements.
columns.validation_strategy: Extract from company websites, LinkedIn company profiles, Crunchbase, or recent news articles. If a range is given, use the midpoint or the most recent figure. For large enterprises, round to the nearest thousand; for startups, round to the nearest ten or hundred as appropriate.

columns.name: Recent funding (last round)
columns.description: Details of the company's most recent funding round, including the amount raised, round type (e.g., Series A, B, C, D, E), date (month/year), and post-money valuation if available. Focus on rounds closed between October 2024 and January 2025.
columns.validation_strategy: Extract from reputable funding databases (e.g., Crunchbase, PitchBook), press releases, or major tech news outlets. Confirm the round closed between October 2024 and January 2025. Include all available details: amount, round type, date, and valuation. If no funding in this window, use the most recent prior round and note the date.[2][4][6][14]

search_strategy.description: Identify a diverse set of AI companies actively hiring for AI roles as of Q4 2024–Q1 2025, spanning research, healthcare, enterprise, and emerging sectors. Include both established leaders and high-growth startups, ensuring recent funding and hiring activity.

subdomains.name: AI Research & Foundation Model Companies
subdomains.focus: Companies focused on core AI research, large language models, and foundational AI technologies (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind, Cohere).
search_queries: foundation model startups hiring AI roles

subdomains.name: Healthcare & Life Sciences AI Companies
subdomains.focus: Companies applying AI to healthcare, drug discovery, medical imaging, and life sciences (e.g., Insilico Medicine, Abridge, Tempus).
search_queries: AI drug discovery companies open positions

subdomains.name: Enterprise & Applied AI Startups
subdomains.focus: Startups and scale-ups building AI-powered enterprise solutions, infrastructure, and automation tools (e.g., Databricks, Dataiku, Runway, Supio).

subdomains.name: Other & Emerging AI Companies
subdomains.focus: Additional AI companies not covered above, including those in legal tech, robotics, creative AI, and new sectors. Captures niche and fast-growing players.

final_rows.match_rationale: Global leader in foundation model research, actively hiring for AI research and engineering roles as of Q4 2024–Q1 2025.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Exceptional match - global AI leader with ChatGPT, actively hiring across research and engineering roles, represents established AI research sector perfectly.

final_rows.match_rationale: Prominent AI safety and research company, known for recruiting top AI talent and maintaining active job postings.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Exceptional match - leading AI safety company with Claude, actively recruiting top talent, represents established AI research sector with strong hiring activity.

final_rows.match_rationale: World-renowned AI research lab, merged with Google Brain, actively hiring across AI research and engineering.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - world-renowned AI research lab, established player with continuous hiring, excellent representation of enterprise/research intersection.

final_rows.match_rationale: AI-driven drug discovery startup focused on cancer, launched in January 2025 with $24.6M funding, actively hiring for research and engineering roles.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Exceptional match - healthcare AI startup launched January 2025 with $24.6M funding, actively hiring, perfectly represents healthcare sector and emerging startup category.

final_rows.match_rationale: European AI research company specializing in open foundation models, actively expanding its team.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - European AI startup with recent funding, actively expanding team, provides geographic and startup diversity to the table.

final_rows.match_rationale: Open-source AI platform and research hub, actively hiring for roles in foundation model development and deployment.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - open-source AI platform with active hiring, represents unique niche in AI infrastructure/tooling, excellent addition for sector diversity.

final_rows.match_rationale: AI research company building foundation models for human-computer interaction, actively recruiting.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - AI startup building foundation models for human-computer interaction, actively recruiting, represents enterprise AI applications and startup segment.

final_rows.match_rationale: AI research lab focused on personal AI assistants, actively hiring for research and engineering roles.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - AI research startup focused on personal assistants, actively hiring, represents consumer AI sector and startup category well.

final_rows.match_rationale: AI research company developing large language models, actively hiring for technical roles.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - AI research company developing LLMs, actively hiring for technical roles, adds diversity to research/enterprise mix.

final_rows.match_rationale: High-growth startup focused on conversational AI and foundation models, with active hiring for research and engineering.
final_rows.qc_rationale: Strong match - high-growth conversational AI startup with active hiring, represents consumer AI sector and startup category effectively.

api_calls.call_description: Finding Rows - AI Research & Foundation Model Companies - Round 1 (sonar-low)
api_calls.prompt_used:
# Row Discovery Task

## Your Job

You are charged with **finding new row entries** for a research table that we are going to build.

These rows are characterized by their **ID fields** (identification columns). Your task is to:

1. **Use web search** to find unique entries that match the specified requirements within the specified subdomain
2. **Populate ID fields** with actual values from your search results
3. **Score each entry** for relevance, source reliability, and recency
4. **Provide reasoning** for why the entry is a good fit

---

## Context About This Table

**User's Original Request:**

I want to create a table tracking AI companies that are actively hiring.

Columns needed:
- Company Name
- Website
- Is hiring for AI roles? (yes/no)
- Team size (approximate)
- Recent funding (last round)

Find about 10 companies across different AI sectors like research, healthcare, and enterprise.
Make sure to include both established companies and startups.


**Table Purpose:**
Identify a diverse set of AI companies actively hiring for AI roles as of Q4 2024–Q1 2025, spanning research, healthcare, enterprise, and emerging sectors. Include both established leaders and high-growth startups, ensuring recent funding and hiring activity.

**Background Research Context:**
This table aims to track leading and emerging AI companies that are actively hiring for AI roles as of late 2024 and early 2025. It incorporates the latest trends in AI hiring, including the surge in demand for both technical and non-technical AI talent, and highlights companies with recent funding activity to ensure organizational growth and hiring capacity. The goal is to provide a cross-sector snapshot of the most dynamic employers in the AI ecosystem.

---

## Your Specific Assignment

**Subdomain:** AI Research & Foundation Model Companies

**Focus Area:** Companies focused on core AI research, large language models, and foundational AI technologies (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind, Cohere).

**Requirements:** Identify a diverse set of AI companies actively hiring for AI roles as of Q4 2024–Q1 2025, spanning research, healthcare, enterprise, and emerging sectors. Include both established leaders and high-growth startups, ensuring recent funding and hiring activity.

**Target:** Find **10 unique entries**

---

## Recommended Searches

Use these search queries to find entries:
- AI research companies hiring 2025
- foundation model startups hiring AI roles
- top AI labs open positions 2025
- AI research company funding rounds 2025

---

## ID Fields to Populate

For each entry you find, populate these ID fields with ACTUAL values from your search:

- **Company Name**: The official name of the AI company as registered or publicly recognized. Used to uniquely identify the organization.
- **Website**: The primary website or careers page of the company, providing direct access to information about the organization and its job openings.

**Example of populated ID fields:**
```json
{
  "Company Name": "Anthropic",
  "Website": "https://anthropic.com"
}
```

**Another example:**
```json
{
  "Company Name": "PathAI",
  "Website": "https://www.pathai.com"
}
```

---

## Scoring Each Entry

Score each entry on three dimensions (0-1.0 scale):

### 1. Relevancy (0-1.0)
How well does this entry match the requirements?
- 1.0 = Perfect match
- 0.7 = Strong match, minor gaps
- 0.4 = Moderate match
- 0.0 = Weak match

### 2. Source Reliability (0-1.0)
How reliable are your information sources?
- 1.0 = Primary sources (official website, Crunchbase, official docs)
- 0.7 = Secondary sources (TechCrunch, LinkedIn, major news outlets)
- 0.4 = Tertiary sources (blogs, forums, aggregators)
- 0.0 = Unreliable or unverified

### 3. Recency (0-1.0)
How recent is the information?
- 1.0 = Less than 3 months old
- 0.7 = 3-6 months old
- 0.4 = 6-12 months old
- 0.0 = More than 12 months or date unknown

---

## Output Format

Return JSON in this exact format:

```json
{
  "subdomain": "AI Research & Foundation Model Companies",
  "candidates": [
    {
      "id_values": {
        "Company Name": "Anthropic",
        "Website": "https://anthropic.com"
      },
      "score_breakdown": {
        "relevancy": 0.95,
        "reliability": 1.0,
        "recency": 0.9
      },
      "match_rationale": "Leading AI safety research company with active hiring for ML engineers",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://anthropic.com/careers",
        "https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/anthropic"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

**Requirements:**
- Use EXACT field names from ID fields list above
- Populate with ACTUAL values from your web search results
- Each entry must be UNIQUE (different from other entries)
- Always include all three dimension scores
- Keep rationale to 1-2 sentences
- Include specific source URLs

---

## Important Notes

- **Find real entries:** Don't make up placeholder names or generic examples
- **Use exact field names:** Copy field names exactly as shown in ID fields list
- **Return candidates array:** Even if empty, always return {"candidates": []}
- **No minimum quality:** Include entries even if scores are moderate (we'll filter later)

Return your findings as valid JSON.

```json
{
```

```json
    {
```

```json
        "reliability": 1.0,

      },

      "source_urls": [
        "https://anthropic.com/careers",
        "https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/anthropic"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

**Requirements:**
- Use EXACT field names from ID fields list above
- Populate with ACTUAL values from your web search results
- Each entry must be UNIQUE (different from other entries)
- Always include all three dimension scores
- Keep rationale to 1-2 sentences
- Include specific source URLs

---

## Important Notes

- **Find real entries:** Don't make up placeholder names or generic examples
- **Use exact field names:** Copy field names exactly as shown in ID fields list
- **Return candidates array:** Even if empty, always return {"candidates": []}
- **No minimum quality:** Include entries even if scores are moderate (we'll filter later)

Return your findings as valid JSON.

Return your findings as valid JSON.

api_calls.call_description: QC Review - Filtering and Prioritizing Rows
